ideal_must_detect.matched_text: would reasonably be expected to materially affect the price of financial products
Parties: Southport Analytics Pty Ltd (buyer) and Harbourlight Telecare Ltd (target) enter this non-binding term sheet for a strategic investment with an option to acquire 60% of issued shares.

must_detect.matched_text: Parties: Southport Analytics Pty Ltd (buyer) and Harbourlight Telecare Ltd (target) enter this non-binding term sheet for a strategic investment with an option to acquire 60% of issued shares.

Parties: Southport Analytics Pty Ltd (buyer) and Harbourlight Telecare Ltd (target) enter this non-binding term sheet for a strategic investment with an option to acquire 60% of issued shares.
Structure and consideration: AUD 28,000,000 in two tranches (Tranche A on signing of SSA; Tranche B on satisfaction of CPs) with an earn-out tied to FY27 EBITDA.
Conditions precedent: board and shareholder approvals, any required ASX Listing Rules relief for change in control, and no objection from ASIC where applicable under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth).
MNPI controls: buyer will not rely on information that is not generally available and would reasonably be expected to materially affect the price of financial products; wall-crossing applies and clean team protocols are in place, and as at 01/05/2026 all clean team notes containing MNPI were destroyed and attested.
Data room and confidentiality: access limited to buyer’s clean team and external counsel; no onward disclosure beyond the information barrier without prior written consent.
Privacy and DPA: Schedule 4 (Data Processing Addendum) to the Share Subscription Agreement will incorporate APPs under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), including APP 3 (collection), APP 8 (cross-border), APP 12/13 (access/correction), and APP 11.2 (destruction/de-identification).
Cross-border transfer: Data Export Approval Memo SEA-2026-04 authorises limited HR payroll metadata processing by Nusantara Compute Pte Ltd (Singapore) from 15/04/2026, with contractual safeguards under APP 8 and onward transfer prohibited without buyer’s written approval.
Data minimisation: for confirmatory diligence, only role titles, salary bands, and anonymised headcount are provided; names, personal addresses, and TFNs are redacted, and no credit card or biometric data are collected.
Access/erasure: two employee access requests (DSAR) under APP 12 received pre-signing were fulfilled on 18/04/2026, and associated timekeeping logs were de-identified on 20/04/2026 in accordance with APP 11.2.
Consent withdrawal: one employee withdrew consent for marketing communications on 22/04/2026; suppression list updated and verified as complete on 23/04/2026.
Health information: onboarding forms may include health disclosures for workplace adjustments (sensitive information) retained onshore only; no health information is authorised for cross-border transfer under this term sheet (policy statement only, not indicating any current transfer).
Credit and onboarding: investor and key supplier onboarding will involve credit checks compliant with the CR Code; credit files are stored separately, not exported, and not included in the data room.
Retention: target’s retention schedule applies (7 years for finance records; 2 years for recruitment files unless required longer by law); no outstanding deletion holds remain as of the date of this term sheet.
Exclusivity and no-shop: 60-day exclusivity with reasonable carve-outs for unsolicited superior proposals; break fee mechanics to be agreed in the SSA.
Notices and contact: primary contact is Ms. Janita Rowe, General Counsel, Harbourlight Telecare Ltd, legal@harbourlight.example.au and privacy@harbourlight.example.au for APP-related notices.
